Article.ArticleTitle: Construction of HIPPEs based on sea bass protein-konjac glucomannan complexes for the delivery of astaxanthin, DHA and EPA.

Abstract.AbstractText: Protein-polysaccharide complexes with different phase behaviors exhibit high potential for stabilizing high internal phase Pickering emulsions (HIPPEs), attracting considerable attention. In this study, HIPPEs loaded with astaxanthin (AST) were prepared using sea bass protein (SBP)- konjac glucomannan (KGM) complexes as the aqueous phase and algal oil (containing docosahexaenoic acid (DHA) and eicosapentaenoic acid (EPA)) as the oil phase. The interfacial cross-linking mechanism of SBP-KGM complexes endowed HIPPEs with excellent environmental stability, as well as favorable viscoelasticity and thixotropic recovery. The residual AST content in HIPPEs was 51.6% higher than that of free AST after storage at 50 °C for 30 days. The bioaccessibility of AST in HIPPEs stabilized by 1.0%SBP-0.2%KGM complexes reached 50.2%. Importantly, the interfacial coating formed by the directional adsorption of SBP-KGM complexes effectively inhibited the oxidation and off-flavor formation in algal oil. The HIPPEs provides new insights and strategies for developing effective carriers for hydrophobic bioactive compounds.
